# Dashboard Polish — Design Spec

> Created 2026-05-30 | Status: Design Complete | Next: writing-plans

## Overview

The dashboard is functionally complete (all 106 tests pass, typecheck clean) but renders as unstyled bare HTML with a text-list graph. This design covers five polish items that bring the dashboard from functional to usable.

**Design system generated by `ui-ux-pro-max`** — data-dense dashboard pattern, blue primary palette, Fira Code + Fira Sans typography.

## Scope

Five polish items in implementation order:

1. **CSS & layout** — CSS Modules + design tokens, grid layout, component styling
2. **Visual graph rendering** — Replace text-list `GraphCanvas` with `@xyflow/react` + dagre auto-layout
3. **Display all artifacts** — Tabbed detail panel showing all 4 simplified artifacts
4. **Pan + scroll-wheel zoom** — Handled automatically by xyflow (built-in)
5. **Interactive comprehension check** — Reveal-on-demand Q&A list in detail panel tab

**Out of scope**: dark mode, graph export, keyboard shortcuts, responsive/mobile layout, loading skeletons, node collapse/expand, virtualized rendering.

## Decisions

| Decision | Chosen | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| CSS approach | CSS Modules | Zero deps, Vite native, scoped by default |
| Graph library | `@xyflow/react` + `@dagrejs/dagre` | React-first, built-in pan/zoom kills item 4, custom node types map to facet kinds |
| Detail panel navigation | Tabs | Clear navigation, one artifact per view, familiar pattern |
| Comprehension check | Reveal-on-demand list | Right balance of interactivity vs complexity, per-question show/hide + global "Show All" |
| Markdown rendering | Minimal inline converter | No library dependency; upgrade to `react-markdown` if needed |
| Layout | `dagre` `rankdir: LR` | Documents on left, concepts/arguments/questions flow rightward in a hierarchical tree |

## Dependencies to Add

```json
{
  "@xyflow/react": "^12.x",
  "@dagrejs/dagre": "^1.x"
}
```

No other new dependencies. CSS Modules is built into Vite. Markdown rendering starts as a minimal inline converter.

## Design System (from ui-ux-pro-max)

| Token | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Primary | `#1E40AF` (blue-800) |
| Secondary | `#3B82F6` (blue-500) |
| CTA / accent | `#F59E0B` (amber-500) |
| Background | `#F8FAFC` (slate-50) |
| Surface | `#FFFFFF` |
| Text primary | `#1E3A8A` (blue-900) |
| Text secondary | `#475569` (slate-600) |
| Text muted | `#94A3B8` (slate-400) |
| Border | `#CBD5E1` (slate-300) |
| Heading font | Fira Code (monospace, 400-700) |
| Body font | Fira Sans (sans-serif, 300-700) |

### Facet Type Colors

| Facet | Border | Badge Background | Purpose |
|-------|--------|-----------------|---------|
| Document | `#1E40AF` | `#DBEAFE` | Primary blue |
| Concept | `#7C3AED` | `#F3E8FF` | Purple |
| Argument | `#F59E0B` | `#FEF3C7` | Amber |
| Question | `#10B981` | `#D1FAE5` | Emerald |

### Edge Type Styling

| Edge Type | Color | Line Style |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| contains | `#94A3B8` (slate-400) | solid |
| defines | `#7C3AED` (purple) | solid, animated |
| depends_on | `#7C3AED` (purple) | dashed, animated |
| exemplifies | `#7C3AED` (purple) | dotted |
| supports | `#F59E0B` (amber) | solid |
| contradicts | `#EF4444` (red-500) | solid, animated |
| questions | `#10B981` (emerald) | dashed |

## Architecture

### Component Changes

```
src/
├── styles/
│   ├── tokens.css              ← NEW: CSS custom properties
│   └── reset.css               ← NEW: box-sizing, margin reset, base font
├── features/
│   ├── graph-canvas.tsx        ← REWRITE: xyflow + dagre replaces text list
│   ├── graph-canvas.module.css ← NEW
│   ├── graph-nodes/
│   │   ├── DocumentNode.tsx    ← NEW: xyflow custom node
│   │   ├── ConceptNode.tsx     ← NEW
│   │   ├── ArgumentNode.tsx    ← NEW
│   │   └── QuestionNode.tsx    ← NEW
│   ├── comprehension-check.tsx  ← NEW: reveal-on-demand Q&A list
│   ├── comprehension-check.module.css ← NEW
│   ├── dashboard-shell.tsx     ← MODIFY: import CSS module, no structural changes
│   ├── dashboard-shell.module.css ← NEW
│   ├── detail-panel-shell.tsx  ← MODIFY: add tab bar, wire 4 artifacts
│   ├── detail-panel-shell.module.css ← NEW
│   ├── search-controls.tsx     ← MODIFY: import CSS module
│   ├── search-controls.module.css ← NEW
│   ├── facet-toggle-group.tsx  ← MODIFY: import CSS module
│   ├── facet-toggle-group.module.css ← NEW
│   ├── source-status-badge.tsx ← MODIFY: import CSS module
│   ├── source-status-badge.module.css ← NEW
│   ├── graph-view-model.ts     ← NO CHANGES (pure logic stays the same)
│   └── markdown-renderer.ts    ← NEW: minimal inline markdown → HTML
├── App.tsx                     ← MODIFY: import reset.css
├── App.module.css              ← NEW
└── main.tsx                    ← NO CHANGES
```

### Unchanged Files

- `graph-view-model.ts` — pure logic layer. xyflow consumes the same `GraphNodeRecord[]` / `GraphEdgeRecord[]` output.
- `load-dashboard-data.ts` — already loads all 4 artifacts.
- `types.ts` — already defines `DashboardSimplifiedDocument` with all 4 fields.
- `create-fixture-source.ts`, `create-workspace-source.ts` — no changes.
- `main.tsx` — no changes.
- All test files — update for xyflow rendering but no new test infrastructure needed.

## Layout Grid

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ HEADER (48px fixed, bg: primary #1E40AF)                │
│ [Logo] [Search bar]          [Source badge] [Refresh]   │
├──────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ SIDEBAR  │ MAIN CONTENT                                 │
│ 240px    │ flex-1                                        │
│          │                                              │
│ Documents│ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ • doc-1  │ │ GRAPH CANVAS (flex-1, xyflow + dagre)   │ │
│ • doc-2  │ │                                          │ │
│ • doc-3  │ │  [doc]──→[concept]──→[argument]          │ │
│          │ │           ↘[question]                    │ │
│ Facets   │ │                                          │ │
│ ☑ Docs   │ └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│ ☑ Conc.  │ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ ☑ Args   │ │ DETAIL PANEL (40vh, min 192px)           │ │
│ ☑ Q's    │ │ ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│          │ │ │ Summary │ Glossary │ Args │ Q&A      │ │ │
│ Source   │ │ ├──────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │
│ /ws/...  │ │ │ Tabbed content area (scrollable)      │ │ │
│          │ │ └──────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
└──────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- Header uses `position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10`
- Body uses `display: flex; height: calc(100vh - 48px)`
- Sidebar uses `width: 240px; flex-shrink: 0; overflow-y: auto`
- Graph canvas uses `flex: 1` (fills remaining vertical space above detail panel)
- Detail panel uses `height: 40vh; min-height: 192px; overflow-y: auto`

## Data Flow

```
loadDashboardData(source)
  ├── knowledge-graph.json → KnowledgeGraphSchema.parse()
  └── per document: loadSimplifiedDocument()
        ├── layered-summary.md    → DashboardSimplifiedDocument.layeredSummary
        ├── concept-glossary.md   → DashboardSimplifiedDocument.conceptGlossary
        ├── argument-map.md       → DashboardSimplifiedDocument.argumentMap
        └── comprehension-check.md → DashboardSimplifiedDocument.comprehensionCheck

App.tsx
  └── visibleData (ReadyDashboardData with documents[].detail.simplified)
        │
        ├── buildGraphViewModel() → nodes, edges, matchedNodeIds
        │     └── GraphCanvas (xyflow) ← consumes nodes + edges
        │
        ├── getDetailSelection() → DetailSelection (concept/argument/question node data)
        │     └── DetailPanelShell (node info bar)
        │
        └── selectedDocument.detail.simplified
              └── DetailPanelShell tabs:
                    ├── Layered Summary   ← simplified.layeredSummary
                    ├── Concept Glossary   ← simplified.conceptGlossary
                    ├── Argument Map       ← simplified.argumentMap
                    └── Comprehension Check ← ComprehensionCheck component (uses doc.questions[] from graph data)
```

## Graph Canvas — xyflow Integration

### Node → xyflow Node Mapping

The existing `buildGraphViewModel()` produces `GraphNodeRecord[]`. We add a transform step:

```typescript
function toXYFlowNodes(records: GraphNodeRecord[]): Node[] {
  return records.map(r => ({
    id: r.id,
    type: `${r.kind}Node`,  // "documentNode", "conceptNode", "argumentNode", "questionNode"
    position: { x: 0, y: 0 }, // dagre fills this
    data: {
      label: r.label,
      kind: r.kind,
      documentId: r.documentId,
      // concept-specific: importance
      // argument-specific: type
      // question-specific: difficulty
    },
  }));
}
```

### Edge → xyflow Edge Mapping

```typescript
function toXYFlowEdges(records: GraphEdgeRecord[]): Edge[] {
  return records.map(e => ({
    id: `${e.source}:${e.target}:${e.type}`,
    source: e.source,
    target: e.target,
    type: "smoothstep",
    animated: ["defines", "depends_on", "contradicts"].includes(e.type),
    style: { stroke: EDGE_COLORS[e.type], strokeDasharray: EDGE_DASH[e.type] },
    label: e.type,
  }));
}
```

### Dagre Layout

Applied on every `buildGraphViewModel` output change (search, facet, data refresh):

```typescript
function applyDagreLayout(nodes: Node[], edges: Edge[]): Node[] {
  const g = new dagre.graphlib.Graph();
  g.setGraph({ rankdir: "LR", nodesep: 60, ranksep: 120, marginx: 40, marginy: 40 });
  g.setDefaultEdgeLabel(() => ({}));
  for (const node of nodes) g.setNode(node.id, { width: 200, height: 56 });
  for (const edge of edges) g.setEdge(edge.source, edge.target);
  dagre.layout(g);
  return nodes.map(n => ({ ...n, position: { x: g.node(n.id).x, y: g.node(n.id).y } }));
}
```

### Custom Node Handles

Each custom node component defines Handles that xyflow uses for edge connection points (guaranteeing edges never cross node bodies):

- **DocumentNode**: `source: Position.Right`, `target: Position.Left`
- **ConceptNode**: `source: Position.Right + Position.Left`, `target: Position.Right + Position.Left`
- **ArgumentNode**: `source: Position.Right + Position.Left`, `target: Position.Right + Position.Left`
- **QuestionNode**: `source: Position.Right + Position.Left`, `target: Position.Right + Position.Left`

## Detail Panel Tabs

### Tab State

Local `useState` in `DetailPanelShell`. Defaults to "summary" tab. Reset to "summary" when `selectedDocumentId` changes.

### Tab Content

| Tab | Source | Rendering |
|-----|--------|-----------|
| Layered Summary | `doc.detail.simplified.layeredSummary` | `<MarkdownRenderer content={...} />` |
| Concept Glossary | `doc.detail.simplified.conceptGlossary` | `<MarkdownRenderer content={...} />` |
| Argument Map | `doc.detail.simplified.argumentMap` | `<MarkdownRenderer content={...} />` |
| Comprehension Check | `doc.questions[]` (from graph, not simplified markdown) | `<ComprehensionCheck questions={doc.questions} />` |

The Comprehension Check tab does **not** render the `comprehension-check.md` markdown directly. It renders the structured `Question[]` array from the document's graph data, using the interactive reveal-on-demand component.

### No Document Selected

When `selectedDocument` is null:
- Tab bar renders but is disabled/greyed out
- Content area shows: "Select a document from the sidebar to view its details."

### Degraded Document

When `document.detail.state === "degraded"`:
- Tab bar renders but only the tabs with available data are clickable
- Each unavailable tab shows: "Content not available: {path}\n{error}"

## Comprehension Check Component

### Props

```typescript
type ComprehensionCheckProps = {
  questions: Question[];  // from DashboardDocument.questions
};
```

### State (internal)

```typescript
const [revealedIds, setRevealedIds] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set());
```

### Behavior

- Lists all questions with difficulty badges (basic=green, intermediate=amber, advanced=red)
- Each question has a "Show Answer" button (hidden by default)
- Clicking "Show Answer" reveals the answer in a green callout box, button changes to "Hide Answer"
- Header bar shows: "N questions | Difficulty: X basic, Y intermediate, Z advanced | Revealed: R of N"
- Header bar has "Show All Answers" / "Hide All Answers" toggle

### No questions

Shows: "No comprehension questions were generated for this document."

## Markdown Renderer

Minimal inline converter. No library dependency.

```typescript
function renderMarkdown(content: string): string {
  // Headings: ### → h4, ## → h4, # → h4
  // Bold: **text** → <strong>
  // Italic: *text* → <em>
  // Code: `text` → <code>
  // Lists: - item → <ul><li>
  // Links: [text](url) → <a>
  // Paragraphs: double newline → <p>
}
```

Used via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` in a `<div>` with scoped CSS. The markdown content comes from pipeline-generated artifacts, not user input, so the XSS risk is minimal.

## Testing Strategy

- **Existing tests** (106) should continue to pass — no structural changes to data flow
- **graph-view-model.test.ts** — no changes needed (pure logic unchanged)
- **graph-canvas.test.tsx** — rewrite tests for xyflow rendering. Use xyflow's `ReactFlowProvider` wrapper. Test node rendering, edge rendering, selection callbacks, search match highlighting.
- **detail-panel-shell.test.tsx** — add tests for tab switching, tab content rendering (markdown + comprehension check), degraded states
- **comprehension-check.test.tsx** — new test file: render questions, show/hide answers, show all/hide all, empty state, difficulty badges
- **markdown-renderer.test.ts** — new test file: headings, bold, italic, code, lists, paragraphs
